message:
feat(core,http): add Link header builder and pipelining tests

Add LinkHeader builder (RFC 8288) with Link/LinkRel types, pagination
helper, title/type params, and apply() for Response. 12 tests.

Add pipelining tests proving StatefulParser already handles multiple
HTTP requests in a single TCP buffer. 2 tests.

Closes bd-1fo8, fastapi_rust-0qq.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1fdj","title":"Test custom exception handlers","description":"## Task
Test that custom exception handlers override default error responses.

## Implementation
- Register custom handlers via ExceptionHandlers
- Test handler receives correct error type
- Test handler can modify response
- Test handler chain priority

## Test Cases
1. Custom handler for ValidationError
2. Custom handler for 404 Not Found
3. Custom handler for 500 Internal Error
4. Handler can log errors
5. Handler can transform error to different format
6. Default handler used when no custom handler registered

## Location

Closing as implemented.","created_at":"2026-01-28T20:32:09Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1hu2","title":"Phase 4: HTTP & Router Integration","description":"## Feature: HTTP & Router Integration

### Parent Epic
Rich Output Integration: Premium Console UI with Agent Compatibility (bd-nww8)

### Overview
This phase integrates rich output capabilities directly into the HTTP parsing and routing layers of fastapi_rust, enabling beautiful debug output for request/response inspection, routing decisions, and validation errors.

### Background & Reasoning
The HTTP and Router crates are the core of request handling. Rich output integration here provides:
- **Parser diagnostics**: Visual HTTP request/response inspection for debugging
- **Routing debug**: See exactly how routes are matched
- **Validation errors**: Beautiful validation failure messages
- **Performance profiling**: Visual timing breakdowns

Unlike application-level output, these components need careful integration to avoid performance impact on the hot path.

### Key Principles for HTTP Layer Integration

1. **Zero Cost When Disabled**: Rich output must have zero runtime cost when not actively debugging
2. **Feature-Gated**: HTTP debug output behind feature flag
3. **No Allocations on Hot Path**: Debug formatters only allocate when actually rendering
4. **Deferred Rendering**: Collect data, render later (not during request processing)

### Components in This Phase

#### 1. HTTP Request Inspector
Visual display of parsed HTTP requests showing:
- Method, path, query string
- Headers in a table
- Body preview (truncated)
- Timing information

#### 2. HTTP Response Inspector
Visual display of HTTP responses showing:
- Status code with color
- Headers
- Body preview
- Size information

#### 3. Routing Debug Output
Shows the routing decision process:
- Which routes were considered
- Why matches failed/succeeded
- Parameter extraction results
- Middleware applied

#### 4. Validation Error Display
Beautiful validation error messages:
- Field path highlighting
- Expected vs received values
- Nested error support
- Multiple errors grouped

### Technical Architecture

#### Feature Gating
```toml
# Cargo.toml
[features]
default = []
debug-output = ["fastapi-output"]
```

```rust
#[cfg(feature = "debug-output")]
pub fn debug_request(req: &Request) {
    RequestInspector::new(req).display();
}

#[cfg(not(feature = "debug-output"))]
pub fn debug_request(_req: &Request) {
    // No-op when feature disabled
}
```

#### Deferred Rendering Pattern
```rust
// Collect data during request processing
struct RequestDebugInfo {
    method: Method,
    path: String,
    headers: Vec<(String, String)>,
    body_preview: Option<String>,
    parse_duration: Duration,
}

// Render only when needed
impl RequestDebugInfo {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        if !output.is_debug_enabled() {
            return;
        }
        // ... render
    }
}
```

### Integration Points

| Component | Integration Point | Output Type |
|-----------|------------------|-------------|
| fastapi-http | Parser completion | Request inspector |
| fastapi-http | Response building | Response inspector |
| fastapi-router | Route matching | Routing debug |
| fastapi-core | Extractor validation | Validation errors |

### Success Criteria
- [ ] HTTP debug output works without affecting production performance
- [ ] Feature flag properly gates all debug functionality
- [ ] Request/response inspection is visually clear
- [ ] Routing debug helps understand matching logic
- [ ] Validation errors are developer-friendly
- [ ] All output respects agent mode

### Dependencies
- Phase 3 complete (bd-1lkf) - builds on advanced components
- RichOutput facade (bd-36uw)
- FastApiTheme (bd-14v4)
- Access to fastapi-http and fastapi-router internals

### Tasks
1. Implement HTTP request inspector
2. Implement HTTP response inspector  
3. Implement routing debug output
4. Implement validation error display","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:14:51.046983969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:35:19.736470706Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:35:19.736398652Z","close_reason":"Implemented HTTP request/response inspectors, routing debug output, and enhanced validation error display in fastapi-output","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hu2","depends_on_id":"bd-1lkf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-0ar","title":"Connection acceptance with limits","description":"Implement connection limiting in server:
- Max concurrent connections limit
- Per-IP connection limits (optional)
- Connection queue with backpressure
- Graceful rejection with 503 when at limit

DEPENDS ON: TCP server with asupersync

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Server respects max_connections setting
- Excess connections rejected gracefully
- No resource exhaustion under load
- Metrics: current connection count

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Limit enforcement
- Integration: Behavior at limit
- Load: Many simultaneous connections
- Recovery: After connections close","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:39:42.844827923Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:17:21.648034840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:17:21.647979135Z","close_reason":"Implemented connection acceptance with limits:
- Added connection_counter AtomicU64 to TcpServer for tracking active connections
- Implemented try_acquire_connection() with compare-exchange for thread-safe limit checking
- Implemented release_connection() for decrementing counter
- Added current_connections() public method for metrics
- Connection limit enforced in accept_loop before handling
- Returns 503 Service Unavailable with Connection: close when limit reached
- Added 8 unit tests for connection limiting behavior","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["limits","phase-1","server"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-0ar","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-9ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-0mi","title":"Content negotiation via Accept headers","description":"Support server-driven content negotiation via Accept, Accept-Encoding, Accept-Language headers. Allow handlers to return different representations based on client preferences.

**Implementation:**
- Parse Accept header with quality values (q=)
- Parse Accept-Encoding for compression negotiation
- Parse Accept-Language for localization
- Content-Type selection based on Accept
- Vary header generation
- 406 Not Acceptable when no match

**Components:**
- AcceptHeader extractor with parsed preferences
- negotiate_content_type() helper
- Vary header auto-generation
- Integration with response serialization

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Accept: application/json,text/html;q=0.9 prefers JSON
- Accept-Encoding respected for compression
- Vary header includes Accept when used
- 406 when requested type not available
- Wildcard matching works

**Tests:**
- Handler returns JSON for Accept: application/json
- Handler returns HTML for Accept: text/html
- Quality values respected in ordering
- 406 for unsupported Accept type
- Vary header included in response","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:12:53.745765535Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:56:54.154932973Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:56:54.154867280Z","close_reason":"Implemented content negotiation via Accept headers:

## Added Types
- MediaType: Parsed media type with type/subtype/parameters
- AcceptItem: Single Accept header entry with quality value  
- AcceptHeader: Full Accept header extractor with negotiation methods
- AcceptEncodingItem/AcceptEncodingHeader: Accept-Encoding support
- AcceptLanguageItem/AcceptLanguageHeader: Accept-Language support
- NotAcceptableError: 406 Not Acceptable error response
- VaryBuilder: Helper for building Vary response headers

## Features
- Parse Accept header with quality values (q=)
- Parse Accept-Encoding for compression negotiation
- Parse Accept-Language for localization
- Content-Type selection based on Accept (negotiate method)
- Quality value comparisons and ordering
- Wildcard matching (*/*, text/*, etc.)
- Vary header generation helper
- 406 Not Acceptable response on negotiation failure

## Tests
- 21 unit tests covering all functionality
- Parse tests for Accept, Accept-Encoding, Accept-Language
- Negotiation tests for content type selection
- Quality value and wildcard matching tests
- VaryBuilder and NotAcceptableError tests

Also added StatusCode::NOT_ACCEPTABLE (406) to response.rs.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","negotiation","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-0mi","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-d6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Implementation:
- Link header builder
- rel types: self, next, prev, first, last, related
- Multiple links in one header
- URL templating support
- Integration with pagination helpers

Link Types:
- next/prev for pagination
- self for canonical URL
- related for associated resources
- alternate for different representations

Acceptance Criteria:
- Multiple links in single header
- Proper RFC 8288 format
- Integration with pagination
- URL encoding handled correctly
- Easy builder API

Tests:
- Pagination adds next/prev links
- Link header properly formatted
- Multiple rel values work
- Special characters in URLs escaped","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:15:13.214282902Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:15:13.214282902Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hateoas","headers","pagination","response"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-0qq","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-rbz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-152","title":"Path extractor implementation","description":"Implement Path<T> extractor:
- Extract path parameters into typed struct
- Support single value: Path<i64>
- Support multiple: Path<(String, i64)>
- Support struct: Path<UserPath>
- Proper error messages on type conversion failure

DEPENDS ON: Router path parameters

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Path<T> works with all supported types
- Clear error for missing/invalid params
- Works with serde derive
- Zero-copy where possible

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Each type extraction
- Unit: Error messages
- Unit: Multiple params
- Integration: With router","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:42:22.325927347Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T06:39:51.632976576Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T06:39:51.632976576Z","close_reason":"Implemented Path<T> extractor with PathParams storage, PathDeserializer for serde integration, support for single values/tuples/structs, comprehensive error handling, and 17 unit tests. Exports added to lib.rs.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extractors","path","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-152","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-cik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-1iw","title":"HTTP parser test suite","description":"Comprehensive test suite for HTTP parser:
- Request line parsing: all methods, various paths, query strings
- Header parsing: standard headers, edge cases, multiline
- Body handling: Content-Length, chunked, empty
- Error cases: malformed requests, truncated input
- Security: smuggling, injection, oversized

TEST CATEGORIES:
1. Valid requests (happy path)
2. Edge cases (empty body, many headers, long values)
3. Invalid requests (proper error responses)
4. Security vectors (known attack patterns)
5. Performance (parsing throughput)

COVERAGE TARGET: 95%+ for parser code","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:39:18.798241577Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T02:32:19.210987732Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T17:17:45.697277936Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","parser","phase-1","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-1iw","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-2l1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
